name: Herb Focaccia
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
2. Grease a cookie sheet.
3. In a large bowl, combine 1 cup flour, sugar, and salt with yeast. Mix well.
4. In a small saucepan, heat water and oil until very warm (120° to 130°F or 50° to 55°C). Remove from heat.
5. Add the warm liquid and egg to the flour mixture. Blend at low speed until moistened. Beat for 2 minutes at medium speed.
6. Stir in an additional 1 3/4 cups flour by hand until the dough pulls away from the sides of the bowl.
7. Cover with a large bowl and let rest for 5 minutes.
8. Place the dough on the greased cookie sheet. Roll or press it into a 12-inch circle.
9. Cover loosely with greased plastic wrap and cloth towel. Let rise in a warm place (80° to 85°F or 27° to 29°C) until doubled in size, about 30 minutes.
10. Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C).
11. Uncover the dough. With fingers or the handle of a wooden spoon, poke holes in the dough at 1-inch intervals.
12. Drizzle 3 to 4 tablespoons olive oil over the top of the dough.
13. Sprinkle evenly with dried rosemary or basil.
14. Bake for 17 to 27 minutes, or until golden brown. Remove from the cookie sheet and cool on a wire rack.
